key_departures.impact: GM John Lynch confirmed Aiyuk played his last snap with the 49ers. The team voided his 2026 guarantees in July 2025 after he stopped communicating with the organization and failed to meet rehab obligations following a torn ACL, MCL, and meniscus. Expected to be released as a post-June 1 designation to spread $29.585M dead cap over two years. Coming off a season in which he played zero snaps.

Led team with 9 TDs in 2025 (55 catches, 643 yards). Had been the 49ers' most reliable receiver over the past two seasons. Spotrac estimated market value ~$22.6M/yr — far beyond SF's comfort zone. Confirmed departing as a UFA. Mike Evans signing effectively replaces him but does not fully compensate for depth lost.

Signed Arizona Cardinals to a 2yr/$10M deal. Had 37 receptions for 551 yards in 2025. Was the team's WR3 and a reliable depth piece in Shanahan's offense. Loss reduces WR depth further; however, not a starter-level departure.
Unrestricted free agent. Was a rotational pass rusher on a team that recorded a league-low 20 sacks in 2025. His departure further depletes an already paper-thin EDGE group.
Unrestricted free agent. Was a depth EDGE rusher. Another pass rush body departing from a unit that desperately needs upgrades.
Starting left guard was a UFA. 49ers already had an interior line need; losing their LG starter widens the hole at the position. Still on the market as of tampering period.
Backup center/IOL depth departed as a UFA. Relatively minor loss but further thins an IOL room that already needs a starter at LG.

Served as Christian McCaffrey's primary backup in 2025. Rushed for 400 yards and 2 TDs. SF has interest in re-signing him per The Athletic, but remains on the market. 49ers have Jordan James and Isaac Guerendo as internal options.
Rotational IDL departed. One of several interior linemen leaving, further hollowing out the DL depth.
Depth IDL departed as a UFA. Minor loss.
Veteran linebacker who was essential when multiple players went down in 2025. His departure thins LB depth behind Fred Warner.
Veteran punter became UFA. The 49ers re-signed Jon Weeks (LS) and Eddy Pineiro (K) but Morstead remains unsigned. Lynch indicated SF was working to re-sign him.

contract.notes: Base contract $42.4M with incentives that could push total value up to $60.4M per Adam Schefter. Structure is team-friendly — per Adam Schefter on Pat McAfee Show, effectively a shorter-term deal structured as 3 years for cap purposes. Only $16.3M guaranteed signals limited real commitment beyond Year 1.

signings.analysis: Evans is one of the most decorated WRs in NFL history (11 consecutive 1,000-yard seasons, 108 career TDs), and the 3-year deal gives SF a legitimate WR1 and red-zone weapon to pair with Ricky Pearsall. However, the low guarantees ($16.3M) and age (32, coming off broken collarbone) mean this is effectively a 1-2 year investment, not a long-term solution. The 49ers are still replacing two starters (Aiyuk, Jennings) with one mid-tier signing. WR remains a critical draft and continued FA priority. Need partially addressed at best — Evans fills Jennings' role in the WR2/red-zone bucket but the room as a whole is still thin.

Lowe is being paid like a backup ($6M AAV, $5.75M guaranteed on a 2-year max deal). He started 25 games over 3 seasons in New England and projects as a swing tackle behind Trent Williams (LT) and Colton McKivitz (RT). The 49ers still have an unresolved contract situation with Williams ($38.84M cap hit in 2026), and Lowe does not solve the OT need — he provides insurance. If Williams is eventually traded or the contract impasse continues, Lowe would be a short-term bridge at LT while a long-term solution is identified. OT need remains, particularly for the future.

Pineiro was outstanding in his first season with SF (28/29 FG, 96.6%), ranking in the top three in field goal percentage since 2000. The 4-year/$17M deal with $10M guaranteed locks him up long-term before he could reach the open market. The 49ers had major kicker instability since Robbie Gould departed after 2022. This fully resolves the K position for the foreseeable future. The deal is signed and prevents any bidding war for a top kicker.
Pro Bowl long snapper re-signed on a 1-year extension. Maintains special teams continuity alongside Pineiro. Low-cost retention of an established starter. Fully resolves LS need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: WR remains the single biggest need despite signing Mike Evans. Evans partially fills Jennings' role at WR2/red zone, but the 49ers lost Aiyuk (starter, released), Jennings (starter, UFA departed), and Bourne (rotational, to Cardinals). Ricky Pearsall (injury-limited in 2025) and Demarcus Robinson are the only other under-contract options. Evans' structure ($16.3M guaranteed, age 32) signals a bridge solution, not a rebuild. Need is elevated. Must draft a WR in Rounds 1-2 and likely add more in FA.

The 49ers posted a league-low 20 sacks in 2025 and a 24.9% pressure rate. Nick Bosa is the only proven pass rusher under contract. They lost Gross-Matos and Ferrell from the rotation without adding a single EDGE. They engaged in bidding for John Franklin-Myers (IDL/EDGE hybrid) but lost him to the Titans at 3yr/$63M. Pass rush remains the most critical defensive need heading into the draft.

Spencer Burford (starting LG) and Matt Hennessy (backup C) both departed as UFAs. No IOL additions confirmed. The interior line already had concerns pre-FA; it has only worsened. LG is now an open starting job. With Trent Williams' contract situation unresolved, even LT depth is fragile. IOL remains a tier-2 need, likely addressed in Rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Vederian Lowe signing ($6M AAV) only minimally addresses the need — he's a swing tackle/backup-caliber signing, not a starter upgrade. Trent Williams' contract impasse ($38.84M cap hit) and age (38 in July) create real uncertainty. If Williams is traded or held out, the starting LT spot is a gaping hole. Need unchanged at tier 2. May need to address early in draft if Williams situation deteriorates.

Jordan Elliott (rotational) and Kevin Givens (depth) both departed. No IDL signings confirmed. The 49ers showed interest in John Franklin-Myers (3yr/$63M, went to Titans). Arik Armstead (restricted to reserve/retired list) and Javon Kinlaw are the primary options. DL depth is thin. Need persists at tier 2 for a pass-rushing IDL complement to Bosa.

No CB additions confirmed. Deommodore Lenoir is locked in long-term but Renardo Green (trade rumors surfaced at Combine) and a thin CB2/CB3 room remain areas of concern. CB need is unchanged from pre-FA.
No safety additions confirmed. Talanoa Hufanga departed to Denver last offseason (2025). The safety group remains thin. Need persists at tier 2.

Eric Kendricks departed. Fred Warner is the elite anchor but depth is thin. Dee Winters, Tatum Bethune, Nick Martin are internal options of varying quality. No LB additions confirmed. Need persists at tier 2 for a quality WILL/depth piece.

Brian Robinson Jr. (top CMC backup, 400 yds/2 TD in 2025) departed as a UFA. SF has Jordan James and Isaac Guerendo internally, but no proven veteran backup. The 49ers have interest in re-signing Robinson per The Athletic. Until a deal is confirmed, RB backup need has escalated from tier 3 to tier 2, as McCaffrey's durability history (multiple injury years) makes a proven backup critical.
Eddy Pineiro re-signed on a 4yr/$17M deal ($10M gtd). Top-3 career FG% since 2000. Fully resolves the K position. Do not draft.
Jon Weeks re-signed on a 1-year extension. Pro Bowl LS retained. Fully resolves need.

key_additions.headline: Future Hall of Famer (108 career TDs, 13,052 yds) arrives as WR1/red-zone anchor, replacing Jennings in a team-friendly incentive-laden deal
26-year-old swing tackle provides insurance behind Williams and RT McKivitz amid ongoing LT contract uncertainty
League's top kicker in 2025 (28/29 FG, 96.6%) locked up long-term, ending years of kicker instability in SF
Pro Bowl long snapper retained, preserving special teams continuity alongside Pineiro

key_losses.contract_summary: Released (post-June 1 expected); ~$13.3M dead cap in 2026, ~$21.2M in 2027
key_losses.headline: Former All-Pro WR never played a snap in 2025 after torn ACL and team communication breakdown; $29.6M dead money hit ends tumultuous tenure
Team's most reliable receiver in 2025 (9 TDs, 55 catches) walks as a UFA after sides couldn't agree on long-term extension; Mike Evans signing effectively confirms his departure
Veteran WR3 and locker room presence signs with division rival Cardinals, further depleting an already thin SF receiver room
Rotational pass rusher departs from a unit that posted a league-low 20 sacks in 2025, deepening the EDGE crisis
Starting left guard departs without a replacement signed, leaving a vacancy at one of SF's most pressing positional needs

key_losses.contract_summary: UFA, SF has interest in re-signing per The Athletic
key_losses.headline: CMC's primary backup (400 yds, 2 TDs in 2025) hits the market; 49ers interested in reunion but no deal confirmed

notes: Free agency officially opened March 11, 2026. All signings (Evans, Lowe, Pineiro, Weeks) were agreed to during the legal tampering window (March 9-11) and are considered confirmed per multiple Tier-1 sources (ESPN/Schefter, NFL Network/Rapoport, Spotrac, OverTheCap). Evans contract base value of $42.4M/$16.3M guaranteed sourced from Ari Meirov/The 33rd Team and confirmed by Spotrac; ESPN initially reported up-to $60.4M including incentives — both figures cited. Brandon Aiyuk release not yet officially processed as of March 11 (Lynch stated it would happen at the start of the new league year with post-June 1 designation likely). Jauan Jennings, Brian Robinson Jr., Spencer Burford remain unsigned and on the market. Trent Williams contract situation (stalled negotiations, trade rumors per Ian Rapoport) unresolved — not reflected in departures as no official transaction has occurred. Cap space estimates based on OverTheCap pre-FA figure of ~$38.7M with adjustments for confirmed signings.
